title: pilosella aurantiaca var. blyttiana
intro: Pilosella aurantiaca var. blyttiana, commonly known as Blytt's hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is a small, perennial plant with bright yellow flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The medicinal properties of Blytt's hawkweed include its ability to reduce inflammation, treat wounds, and support the immune system, providing health benefits such as reduced pain and improved wound healing. Blytt's hawkweed can be prepared as a tincture, salve, or infusion, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used in the treatment of various health conditions.
